tools.name: audit_visibility
tools.description: Run an AI-visibility audit for a business (requires an Incisory account — every account includes one free audit, which runs at signup; paid accounts can re-run freely). Pass just the website and Incisory reads the site to work out what it sells. Asks ChatGPT, Gemini and Perplexity the questions its

tools.name: get_audit
tools.description: Fetch a previously run audit by its id (from the shareable result URL).

tools.name: visibility_history
tools.description: Visibility trend across your weekly snapshots. Uses your account (OAuth or token) — you can only read your own history.

tools.name: my_visibility
tools.description: For a subscribed Incisory client: latest AI-visibility snapshot + trend, using your account token.

tools.name: my_report
tools.description: Full latest weekly report for a subscribed client (verbatim answers, quick wins) using your account token.

tools.name: find_reddit_opportunities
tools.description: Search for real, high-intent Reddit threads relevant to a business and draft a disclosed, helpful reply for each — queued for human review, never auto-posted (posting is your call, from your own account). Use your account token, or pass business/website/niche directly for an ad-hoc search.

tools.name: list_reddit_queue
tools.description: List pending Reddit opportunities (copy-paste drafts + thread links) for your account.

tools.name: mark_reddit_opportunity
tools.description: After you post a drafted reply yourself (or decide to skip it), mark it here so it stops appearing in your queue.

tools.name: list_earned_mentions
tools.description: List pending outreach pitches (white-hat, drafted for you to send) to the sources AI already cites instead of your account — never auto-sent.

tools.name: mark_earned_mention
tools.description: After you send a drafted pitch yourself (or decide to skip it), mark it here so it stops appearing in your queue.

tools.name: ai_traffic
tools.description: Show how many visits AI assistants sent the client (last 30 days) + value, and the one-line tracking snippet to install.

tools.name: write_article
tools.description: Generate a genuinely good, branded article designed to get cited by AI assistants for a specific buyer question. Returns a draft (title + body + FAQ schema) stored in your content queue for review — not auto-published. Use your account token, or pass business/website/niche for an ad-hoc draft.

tools.name: list_content_queue
tools.description: List generated article drafts (title, target question, status) for your account.

tools.name: mark_content
tools.description: After you publish a draft yourself (or decide to skip it), mark it so it leaves the queue.

tools.name: changelog
tools.description: List the automated work Incisory has done for the account (scans, articles written, Reddit threads found) over the last N days.

tools.name: competitor_decode
tools.description: The latest competitor decode for the account: why AI engines cite the top rival instead of you — their footprint (matchable vs out of reach) and the specific moves you can make this quarter. Runs weekly on the paid plan.

tools.name: brand_accuracy
tools.description: The latest brand-accuracy scan: what AI assistants currently state about the business, with each claim checked against the real web (correct / outdated / likely-wrong / missing) and the highest-leverage fix. Runs weekly on the paid plan.

tools.name: set_autonomy
tools.description: Choose how much Incisory does on its own each week: "notify" (just tell me), "approve" (draft and wait for me), or "auto" (do it all).

tools.name: track_prompt
tools.description: Add a question your buyers ask (e.g. "best CRM for small law firms") to your tracked set — it gets re-asked across ChatGPT/Gemini/Perplexity every weekly scan so you see a real per-question trend. Max 12 tracked questions.

tools.name: list_prompts
tools.description: The questions Incisory re-asks the AI engines every week for your account, with each engine's latest verdict.

tools.name: citation_gap
tools.description: Show which third-party sources (review sites, directories, communities, Wikipedia, etc.) ChatGPT/Gemini actually cited while answering questions where you were NOT named — a prioritized list of exactly where to get listed, reviewed, or mentioned next. Use your account token (uses your latest scan),

tools.name: google_data
tools.description: Real (not estimated) Google Search Console clicks/impressions/ranking-position and GA4 sessions, plus verified AI-referral session counts, for your connected Google account. If not connected yet, returns the link to connect. Uses your account token.

tools.name: upgrade_plan
tools.description: Get the Stripe checkout link to subscribe — one plan, €49/mo, everything included, with a free 3-day trial.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: get_audit, find_reddit_opportunities, list_reddit_queue, write_article, changelog, citation_gap · Private data: visibility_history, my_visibility, my_report, find_reddit_opportunities, write_article, citation_gap, google_data · Public sink: mark_reddit_opportunity, list_earned_mentions, mark_earned_mention, mark_content, set_autonomy · Destructive: audit_visibility, get_audit.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: get_audit, find_reddit_opportunities, list_reddit_queue, write_article, changelog, citation_gap · Private data: visibility_history, my_visibility, my_report, find_reddit_opportunities, write_article, citation_gap, google_data · Public sink: mark_reddit_opportunity, list_earned_mentions, mark_earned_mention, mark_content, set_autonomy · Destructive: audit_visibility, get_audit

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
